assertions.assertion: Output sets ForwardLimit to 2 (or the correct number matching the proxy chain depth), explaining that the default of 1 only processes headers from a single proxy
assertions.rationale: ForwardLimit for multiple proxies is documented in ASP.NET Core docs and general knowledge.

assertions.assertion: Output adds both proxy IP addresses to KnownProxies or their network ranges to KnownNetworks
assertions.rationale: Adding proxy IPs to KnownProxies is standard ASP.NET Core knowledge.

assertions.assertion: Output includes ForwardedHeaders.XForwardedHost in addition to XForwardedProto to fix the incorrect host name
assertions.rationale: A general LLM might focus on XForwardedFor and XForwardedProto but miss the XForwardedHost flag specifically for the host name issue.

assertions.assertion: Output explains that each proxy in the chain must be trusted (added to KnownProxies/KnownNetworks) for the forwarded headers to be processed
assertions.rationale: A general LLM might not explicitly explain the trust chain requirement for each proxy.
